project_title_en: Automate the review of non-complex applications for Temporary Resident Visas and Work Permits made under the Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel (CUAET)
project_title_fr: Automatiser l'examen des demandes non complexes de visa de résident temporaire et de permis de travail faites dans le cadre de l'Autorisation de voyage d'urgence Canada-Ukraine (AVUCU)
description_en: The CUAET program was designed from the beginning to respond urgently to the crisis in Ukraine by streamlining our existing temporary resident process. IRCC will now further enhance efficiency by automating certain checks and actions previously performed by IRCC officers. For CUAET applications, these tasks and activities now include automatically confirming a positive decision and generating a visa if all eligibility and admissibility criteria are met. Applicants will still be required to take all the usual steps to complete their application, including enrolling their biometrics.  An application that does not pass automated checks will be sent to an officer for a manual review. An application sent for manual review may still be approved, but only an IRCC officer can refuse an application. The system never refuses or recommends refusing applications, and does not use artificial intelligence or advanced analytics.
description_fr: Ce projet permettra d'automatiser le traitement des demandes pour certains visas de résident temporaire et permis de travail faites dans le cadre de l'Autorisation de voyage d'urgence Canada-Ukraine (AVUCU). Dès le départ le programme AVUCU a été conçu pour répondre de toute urgence à la crise en Ukraine en rationalisant notre processus de résidence temporaire existant. IRCC améliorera l'efficacité en automatisant certains examens et actions précédemment effectués par les agents d'IRCC. Pour les candidatures de l’AVUCU, ces tâches et activités incluent la confirmation automatique d'une décision positive et la génération d'un visa si tous les critères de recevabilité et d'admissibilité sont remplis. Les candidats devront toujours suivre toutes les étapes habituelles pour compléter leur demande, y compris l'inscription.

The system is expected to have a low impact on the rights and freedoms of individuals as it will solely be used to automate positive decisions and take administrative steps. The system rules use data elements with a clear link to legislative and regulatory requirements. 

The system never refuses applications nor does it recommend refusals, merely indicate the need for manual review. All refusals continue to be done by officers as per the current practice. All applications for which assessments cannot be approved by the system will receive an individualized assessment by officers in accordance with standard practice. All refusals continue to be made by officers. Applications which are removed from the automation process due to the triggering of a rule may still be approved by officers.

Rules have all been reviewed by key stakeholders in IRCC across policy, operations, legal, and privacy areas. Regular monitoring and quality assurance measures will also help make sure the system continues to perform as intended and that any unforeseen negative impacts such as bias or discrimination can be identified early and mitigated.
